name: ThemeRules
description: Theme-specific composition and layout rules for Harmony Design System

overview.majorDifference: CP uses FloatingNav, others use Footer

cp.name: Costpoint
uniqueFeatures: 88px top padding on main content (to accommodate FloatingNav)

conditional.FloatingNav: CP theme only - positioned below header
conditional.ShellPanel: When leftPanel or rightPanel props are provided

vp.name: Vantagepoint
uniqueFeatures: 20px top padding on main content (less than CP)

conditional.ShellPanel: When leftPanel or rightPanel props are provided

ppm.name: Project Performance Management
uniqueFeatures: Footer component for navigation (same as VP)

conditional.ShellPanel: When leftPanel or rightPanel props are provided

maconomy.name: Maconomy
uniqueFeatures: Footer component for navigation (same as VP/PPM)

conditional.ShellPanel: When leftPanel or rightPanel props are provided

allThemes: All support light and dark modes
All have ShellHeader at top (56px height)
Same side padding formula on desktop (72px)

componentLevelDifferences.overview: Component-level styling differences between CP and other themes. These defaults are implemented in CSS and not visible in component JSON metadata.

formLabelDefaults.description: CP theme defaults to inline (horizontal) label layout, while VP/PPM/Maconomy default to stacked (vertical)

cp.behavior: When labelVariant prop is null/undefined, CSS applies inline layout automatically

standard.behavior: Base wrapper styles default to flex-direction: column (stacked)

componentSizes.description: CP theme uses compact component heights (20px) while other themes use standard heights (40px)

darkModeSchemes.description: CP uses a 'semi-dark' color scheme with lighter backgrounds, while VP/PPM/Maconomy use a 'full dark' scheme with nearly black backgrounds

cp.characteristics: Lighter backgrounds, more gray tones, softer contrast for better readability

standard.characteristics: Darker backgrounds, nearly black, higher contrast

lightMode.description: All four themes use identical light mode colors - no differences between CP, VP, PPM, and Maconomy in light mode

cpExclusiveComponents.description: Components that are exclusive to CP theme or have CP-specific styling

FloatingNav.description: Navigation bar positioned below header, replaces ShellFooter in CP theme
FloatingNav.positioning: Fixed, below header at 56px, horizontally centered

uniqueColors.note: FloatingNav buttons use unique colors distinct from theme primary #4C92D9

CPLeftSidebar.behavior: Expands on hover to show labels. Labels hidden by default (opacity: 0, visibility: hidden)

CPRightSidebar.behavior: Expands on hover to show labels. Labels hidden by default (opacity: 0, visibility: hidden)

excludedComponents.description: Components that should NOT be used in certain themes

ShellFooter.reason: CP uses FloatingNav for navigation instead of footer

rules.rule: Layout adapts based on HTML class (theme-cp, theme-vp, theme-ppm, theme-maconomy)
rules.rationale: Single layout component handles all themes via CSS classes

rules.rule: CP theme MUST include FloatingNav and MUST NOT include Footer
rules.rationale: CP uses different navigation pattern than other themes

rules.rule: VP/PPM/Maconomy themes MUST include Footer and MUST NOT include FloatingNav
rules.rationale: Standard themes use footer for navigation

examples.incorrect: VP: ShellHeader + FloatingNav + main (should use Footer)

rules.rule: CP theme requires 88px top padding to accommodate FloatingNav
rules.rationale: FloatingNav sits below header, needs space

rules.rule: VP/PPM/Maconomy themes use 3-row grid (header, main, footer)
All components must work in all 4 themes × 2 modes (8 combinations)
rules.rationale: Ensures design system consistency across products

rules.rule: All themes share same responsive breakpoints
Hide left and right sidebars on mobile (≤768px) for all themes
rules.rationale: Maximize content area on small screens

rules.rule: CP theme defaults to inline labels for form inputs, VP/PPM/Maconomy default to stacked
rules.rationale: CP's compact design philosophy requires horizontal label layout to save vertical space
rules.override: Use labelVariant="stacked" prop to override in CP, labelVariant="inline" in other themes

rules.rule: CP theme uses 20px height for inputs/dropdowns, other themes use 40px
rules.rationale: CP requires compact UI for dense data entry workflows

rules.rule: CP uses semi-dark color scheme, VP/PPM/Maconomy use full-dark scheme
rules.rationale: CP's semi-dark provides better readability for long work sessions, while full-dark provides maximum contrast
rules.note: Light mode colors are identical across all themes

rules.rule: FloatingNav is CP-exclusive, ShellFooter is excluded from CP
rules.rationale: Different navigation patterns: CP uses FloatingNav below header, others use footer

floatingNavColors.primaryButton: #ff507b (pink/red - unique to FloatingNav, NOT theme primary)

rules.rule: CP sidebars expand from 52px to 220px on hover to reveal labels
rules.rationale: Saves horizontal space while providing access to full navigation labels on demand
rules.labelBehavior: Hidden by default (opacity: 0, visibility: hidden), visible on hover

decisionLog.decision: CP uses FloatingNav below header instead of footer
decisionLog.rationale: Costpoint navigation pattern differs from other products
decisionLog.impact: CP requires different grid structure and top padding (88px vs 20px)

decisionLog.decision: Single ShellLayout component handles all themes via CSS classes
decisionLog.impact: Theme switching via HTML class, conditional rendering of FloatingNav/Footer

decisionLog.decision: All themes use same primary color (#4C92D9)
decisionLog.impact: Theming differences are mainly in layout composition, not colors

decisionLog.decision: All themes use same sidebar width (52px)
decisionLog.impact: Icon-based navigation in sidebars, tooltips for labels

whenBuildingShellLayout: 1. Detect theme from HTML class or context
3. Conditionally include FloatingNav (CP only) or Footer (VP/PPM/Maconomy)
4. Apply correct top padding: 88px for CP, 20px for others
5. Set grid-template-rows: 2 rows for CP, 3 rows for others
6. Ensure all conditional components (ShellPageHeader, ShellPanel) work in all themes
whenBuildingFormComponents: 1. Detect theme from context to determine label variant default
2. CP theme: Labels default to inline (horizontal) unless labelVariant="stacked" is specified
3. VP/PPM/Maconomy: Labels default to stacked (vertical) unless labelVariant="inline" is specified
4. Let CSS handle the default behavior - only use labelVariant prop to override
5. Apply correct component heights: 20px for CP (--input-height-cp), 40px for others
6. Use correct font sizes: --text-xs (12px) for CP, --text-sm (14px) for others
whenApplyingDarkMode: 1. Check if theme is CP or standard (VP/PPM/Maconomy)
2. CP dark mode: Use semi-dark scheme (--page-bg: #1F252E, --card-bg: #37424D)
3. Standard dark mode: Use full-dark scheme (--page-bg: #15171A, --card-bg: #1F2124)
4. Light mode: All themes use identical colors - no theme-specific overrides needed
5. Never hardcode color values - always use CSS custom properties (tokens)
whenUsingCPExclusiveComponents: 1. FloatingNav: Only render in CP theme, positioned below header
2. FloatingNav buttons: Use unique colors (#ff507b for primary, NOT theme primary)
3. CPLeftSidebar/CPRightSidebar: Only use in CP theme, standard themes use LeftSidebar/RightSidebar
4. CP sidebars: Default 52px width, expand to 220px on hover with labels appearing
5. ShellFooter: Never include in CP theme layouts
testingChecklist: Test light and dark modes for each theme
Verify FloatingNav appears only in CP theme
Verify Footer appears only in VP/PPM/Maconomy themes
Verify correct top padding for each theme
Verify sidebars hide on mobile for all themes
Verify form labels default to inline in CP, stacked in others
Verify component heights: 20px in CP, 40px in others
Verify dark mode colors: semi-dark for CP, full-dark for others
Verify CP sidebars expand on hover (52px → 220px)

antiPatterns.pattern: Hardcoding 88px padding instead of checking theme
antiPatterns.instead: Use theme-aware padding: CP gets 88px, others get 20px
Conditionally render Footer based on theme
Conditionally render FloatingNav only for CP theme

antiPatterns.pattern: Creating different color palettes per theme
antiPatterns.why: Primary colors are same across themes
antiPatterns.instead: Focus on layout/composition differences, not color changes

antiPatterns.pattern: Using 40px input heights in CP theme
antiPatterns.why: CP theme requires compact 20px heights for dense workflows
antiPatterns.instead: Use --input-height-cp (20px) and --text-xs (12px) font size for CP theme

antiPatterns.pattern: Defaulting to stacked labels in CP theme or forcing inline in other themes
antiPatterns.why: CP defaults to inline labels for compact layout, other themes default to stacked
antiPatterns.instead: Let CSS handle defaults based on theme, only use labelVariant prop to override
antiPatterns.correct: CP: no labelVariant (inline by default), VP/PPM/Maconomy: no labelVariant (stacked by default)

antiPatterns.pattern: Including ShellFooter component in CP theme layouts
antiPatterns.why: CP uses FloatingNav for navigation, not footer
antiPatterns.instead: Conditionally render ShellFooter only for VP/PPM/Maconomy themes
antiPatterns.correct: CP: ShellHeader + FloatingNav + main, Others: ShellHeader + main + ShellFooter

antiPatterns.pattern: Including FloatingNav in VP/PPM/Maconomy theme layouts
antiPatterns.why: FloatingNav is exclusive to CP theme
antiPatterns.instead: Only render FloatingNav when theme is CP, use ShellFooter for other themes

antiPatterns.pattern: Using full-dark color values (#15171A, #1F2124) in CP dark mode
antiPatterns.why: CP uses semi-dark scheme with lighter backgrounds for better readability
antiPatterns.note: Light mode colors are identical across all themes, no need for theme-specific overrides
